$comment: Germany column of the comparative rights-map. Same 30-UDHR-invariant spine as the UK map; backing = the Grundgesetz (Basic Law). Distinctive-article text retrieved from the official English translation at gesetze-im-internet.de (web, not an API — a different provenance tier from the UK's Lex source). Two contrasts with the UK are the whole point: (1) COVERAGE is wider — Germany constitutionally backs freedom of movement (Art 11), asylum (Art 16a) and nationality (Art 16), none of which the UK HRA covers, plus a judicially-derived subsistence minimum (Existenzminimum, Art 1+20); (2) REMEDY is far stronger — see meta.remedy.

meta.name: Germany

instrument.title: Grundgesetz fuer die Bundesrepublik Deutschland (Basic Law)

meta.remedy_note: The Bundesverfassungsgericht can VOID a law (abstract and concrete judicial review). Art 79(3) — the eternity clause — puts human dignity (Art 1) and the democratic/social order (Art 20) beyond amendment even by a two-thirds supermajority. So the guard here can strike, and the core cannot be repealed at all. Germany also practises 'militant democracy' (streitbare Demokratie): parties aiming to abolish the free democratic order can be banned (GG Art 21(2)), and rights forfeited (Art 18).

invariants.udhr: Art 1 — free and equal in dignity
invariants.right: GG Art 1 — human dignity, inviolable (unamendable via Art 79(3))
GG Art 3 — equality before the law
invariants.text: All persons shall be equal before the law.

invariants.right: GG Art 2(2) — life, physical integrity, liberty
invariants.text: Every person shall have the right to free development of his personality...

invariants.right: GG Art 12(2)-(3) — prohibition of forced labour
GG Art 1(1) dignity + Art 104(1) (no abuse of detainees)
GG Arts 1-2 — legal personality via dignity
GG Art 3 — equality before the law
invariants.text: All persons shall be equal before the law.

invariants.right: GG Art 19(4) — recourse to the courts (strong, explicit)
invariants.text: Should any person's rights be violated by public authority, he may have recourse to the courts.

invariants.right: GG Art 2(2) + Art 104 — deprivation-of-liberty safeguards
GG Art 103(1) right to be heard + Art 101 lawful judge

invariants.udhr: Art 11 — presumption of innocence; no retroactive law
invariants.right: GG Art 103(2) — nulla poena sine lege
GG Art 10 (correspondence) + Art 13 (home) + Art 2(1) informational self-determination
invariants.text: The privacy of correspondence, posts and telecommunications shall be inviolable.

invariants.right: GG Art 11 — freedom of movement
invariants.text: All Germans shall have the right to move freely throughout the federal territory.

invariants.right: GG Art 16a — right of asylum (explicit constitutional right)
invariants.text: Persons persecuted on political grounds shall have the right of asylum.

invariants.right: GG Art 16 — no deprivation of citizenship
invariants.text: No German may be deprived of his citizenship.

invariants.right: GG Art 6 — special protection of marriage and family
GG Art 14 — property and inheritance guaranteed
invariants.text: Property and the right of inheritance shall be guaranteed.

invariants.right: GG Art 4 — freedom of faith and conscience, inviolable
invariants.text: Freedom of faith and of conscience and freedom to profess a religious or philosophical creed shall be inviolable.

invariants.right: GG Art 5 — expression, press, arts, sciences
invariants.text: Every person shall have the right freely to express and disseminate his opinions in speech, writing and pictures...

invariants.right: GG Art 8 (assembly) + Art 9 (association, incl. unions)
invariants.text: All Germans shall have the right to assemble peacefully and unarmed without prior notification or permission.

invariants.right: GG Art 38 (general, direct, free, equal, secret) + Art 20(2)
invariants.text: Members of the German Bundestag shall be elected in general, direct, free, equal and secret elections.

invariants.right: GG Art 20(1) Sozialstaatsprinzip — social-state principle (state objective, not an enumerated individual right)
invariants.text: The Federal Republic of Germany is a democratic and social federal state.

invariants.right: GG Art 12 (occupational freedom) + Art 9(3) (union freedom, collective bargaining)
(no constitutional counterpart)
Existenzminimum — a subsistence minimum the BVerfG derives from GG Art 1 + Art 20 (Hartz IV, 2010)
GG Art 7 — the entire school system under state supervision
GG Art 5(3) — freedom of the arts and sciences (production, not participation)
GG Art 20 — democratic and social federal state (programmatic)
invariants.text: The Federal Republic of Germany is a democratic and social federal state.

invariants.right: GG Art 2(1) — rights limited by the rights of others and the constitutional order
GG Art 18 (forfeiture of basic rights — militant democracy) + Art 79(3) eternity clause
